close_reason: connected_client() now returns Result instead of panicking with expect()
description: lib.rs:88-92 connected_client() does state.client.as_mut().expect('called before ensure_connected'). Every helper docstring repeats // Panics: call ensure_connected first. Rust expresses 'this value is connected' in the type system: ensure_connected could return &mut Client directly so callers cannot misuse the API. Cross-references the P3 elegance finding — same code, idiom-level framing here. A serious Rust reviewer reads this and asks why a runtime check guards an invariant the compiler would happily enforce.
title: WolfHsmState::connected_client uses runtime expect() instead of typestate; not idiomatic Rust
